spec_match.rationale: Page demonstrates the headline Add presentationTime/paintTime to performance entries surface (paintTime, presentationTime, LCP); residual gaps in spec vocabulary: implementation-defined.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button, eventListener); controls include "Inject LCP candidate"; "Add element-timing target"; "Force first-paint" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: One failure-mode signal is present but most edge cases (errors, unsupported environment, empty/zero inputs) are not demonstrated.

references.rationale: The concept page now includes the required canonical ChromeStatus self-heal link https://chromestatus.com/feature/5162859838046208.

design_system.rationale: Design-system regressions: raw hex colours present in <style>; button rule overrides background without color (.trig-btn:hover).

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on rum-beacon-builder
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

openQuestions.title: Bring rum-beacon-builder back onto the design system
openQuestions.detail: Design-system regressions: raw hex colours present in <style>; button rule overrides background without color (.trig-btn:hover). See CLAUDE.md rules #5, #6, #7 — CSS variables only, WCAG AA, button background+color always overridden together.

summary: rum-beacon-builder for Add presentationTime/paintTime to performance entries has structural gaps: the rubric flags multiple fails (see open questions). Needs a pass to convert it from a placeholder into a real concept demo. ChromeStatus reference link resolved.
